subject: Ecology
title: Madagascar
description: A fast breakdown of Madagascar for builders.

hook.text: Madagascar split from Africa 160 million years ago and became evolution's private workshop.

buildup.text: Lemurs, chameleons, and baobabs evolved there in isolation, filling niches occupied elsewhere by others.

discovery.text: Over 90% of Madagascar's reptiles and plants are found nowhere else on the planet.

twist.text: Humans arrived only 2,000 years ago and have since destroyed 90% of the original forest.

climax.text: Madagascar is simultaneously one of the richest and most threatened places on Earth.

punchline.text: A continent's worth of evolution on one island.

quiz.question: When did Madagascar separate from Africa?
